title: Rivers and Ashtrays
summary:
Our next destination was some dispersed camping outside of Escalante. On our way we stopped to do a hike up the Escalante River which was a trail that meandered up the canyon, crossing the river multiple times. We only saw one other person out there, a nomad and his dog who were heading to the car for a resupply before going back out to camp for more nights since there were so many cool canyons to explore. After our hike we stopped at the little grocery store in Escalante and treated ourselves to $0.99 soft serve.

We headed up to Spencer Flat road, a very cool spot, with lots of gorgeous dispersed camping and canyon views in every direction. We spent two nights there, taking the next day to hike the Cosmic Ashtray, which we heard about through my brother Joe and his girlfriend Caeley, who visited the area last year. The hike was mostly on slickrock, and without the phone telling us where the path was, I don't think we would ever have found it. The Cosmic Ashtray is a gigantic bowl filled with dune-like sand that you can climb down into. It was way bigger and way more impressive than I had imagined. The next day we did one more hike along the Escalante but this time from the other side of the canyon than we were a few days ago. We saw some petroglyphs and a big natural bridge and Maya enjoyed the river crossings.

-Helen
